skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through the inference.sh CLI. Supports text prompts, image edits, multiple outputs, aspect ratios, resolution settings, and optional Google Search grounding.
skill.summary: Run Nano Banana 2 image generation and editing through inference.sh.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell execution patterns and hardcoded URLs, plus high-severity weak-cryptography matches. Review found the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary words and table text, while the shell and network behavior is real but expected for a CLI-based image-generation skill. No prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Sends User Content to Image Services
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed around Bash execution of the infsh CLI. These commands submit prompts and optional input images to the inference.sh service and a Google image model, which is expected behavior but requires user awareness before sharing sensitive content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration permits infsh Bash commands, and the examples clearly invoke a remote image-generation app. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates real external command and data-sharing risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation and an example image URL. They do not show credential exfiltration or hidden callbacks, but they confirm that users will interact with third-party network services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and a placeholder input image URL. No evidence found of concealed tracking endpoints or credential-bearing request construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported high-severity cryptography matches occur in normal text such as the skill description, a logo design prompt, and table headers. No cryptographic algorithm use was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language words and Markdown table text, not crypto APIs or algorithm configuration. This strongly supports dismissal of the static blocker findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Allowlist for infsh Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash execution for infsh commands. This is scoped to the intended CLI, but marketplace users should know that prompts and file references may be processed outside the local workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool allowlist and quick-start command are explicit. The risk is bounded by the infsh pattern, but command execution is still a meaningful capability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Google Search Grounding
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents an enable_google_search option for real-time grounding. This can send prompt context to external services and may retrieve current web information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The option is documented directly in the input examples and parameter table. It is an intentional feature, not hidden behavior.

content.user_title: Generate Gemini Images with Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Design teams need fast image drafts and edits without switching tools. This skill runs Gemini image generation through inference.sh from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Runs the inference.sh CLI for Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview.
Generates images from text prompts with selectable aspect ratios.
Edits existing images supplied through image inputs or image URLs.
Requests multiple generated images in a single run.
Supports 1K, 2K, and 4K resolution settings documented by the skill.
Can enable Google Search grounding for prompts that need current context.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external services for processing.
Image editing depends on supported input formats and remote model behavior.
The skill documents usage examples but does not implement local image generation.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Visual Drafts
use_cases.description: Generate multiple visual directions for social posts, landing pages, or ads from concise creative prompts.

use_cases.title: Edit Product Images
use_cases.description: Modify existing product images by adding backgrounds, changing composition, or testing seasonal styling.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Concepts
use_cases.description: Create quick visual references for feature ideas, storyboard frames, or presentation concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to generate one square image of [subject] in [style]. Keep the prompt concise and use 1K resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Create Several Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to create four versions of [scene]. Use a [ratio] aspect ratio and vary lighting, color, and composition.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to edit this image: [image URL or file reference]. Change [specific element] while preserving [important details].

prompt_templates.title: Ground a Current Visual
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 with Google Search grounding to visualize [current topic]. Make the result accurate, polished, and suitable for [audience].

output_examples.input: A designer asks for four poster concepts for a new coffee shop.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs Nano Banana 2 with a logo or poster prompt, requests four images, and returns the generated image results with a short description.

output_examples.input: A store owner provides a product photo and asks for a summer background.
output_examples.output: The assistant submits the image for editing, keeps the product unchanged, and returns an edited image with the requested seasonal scene.

output_examples.input: A product manager asks for a current-weather visual for Tokyo.
output_examples.output: The assistant enables Google Search grounding, generates an image based on current context, and explains that external grounding was used.

best_practices: Review prompts and image inputs for sensitive information before running the CLI.
Specify aspect ratio, resolution, style, and important preserved details in each request.
Use multiple outputs when exploring concepts, then refine the strongest result.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images, private documents, or credentials as prompt content.
Do not expect deterministic image results from repeated prompts.
Do not enable Google Search grounding when current web context is unnecessary.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code run Nano Banana 2 image generation through the inference.sh CLI.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires inference.sh CLI access and sends generation requests to external image services.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image input support for editing JPEG, PNG, and WebP images.

faq.question: How many input images can it use?
faq.answer: The skill states that image editing can use up to 14 input images.

faq.question: Can it generate high-resolution images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented resolution options include 1K, 2K, and 4K.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image inputs may be processed by third-party services, so avoid sensitive content.
